input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# generate-matrix.sh — read a matrix config JSON and emit an expanded
# GitHub Actions build matrix as JSON.
#
# Config schema:
#   {
#     "axes":         { "<key>": ["v1","v2",...], ... },
#     "include":      [ {<key>: <val>, ...}, ... ],
#     "exclude":      [ {<key>: <val>, ...}, ... ],
#     "max-parallel": <int|null>,
#     "fail-fast":    <bool>,
#     "max-size":     <int|null>
#   }
#
# Output:
#   { "matrix": {"include": [...]}, "count": N,
#     "max-parallel": ..., "fail-fast": ... }
# Exit codes: 0 ok, 2 bad input, 3 matrix exceeds max-size.
set -euo pipefail

usage() { echo "usage: $0 <config.json>   (or pipe JSON to stdin with -)" >&2; }

src="${1:-}"
if [[ -z "$src" ]]; then
    usage; exit 2
fi
if [[ "$src" == "-" ]]; then
    input="$(cat)"
elif [[ -f "$src" ]]; then
    input="$(cat "$src")"
else
    echo "Error: config file not found: $src" >&2
    exit 2
fi

if ! jq -e . >/dev/null 2>&1 <<<"$input"; then
    echo "Error: invalid JSON in config" >&2
    exit 2
fi
if ! jq -e '(.axes // null) | (type == "object") or (. == null)' >/dev/null <<<"$input"; then
    echo "Error: 'axes' must be an object" >&2
    exit 2
fi

# The jq program below does the real work: cartesian product of axes,
# apply excludes (an exclude rule matches a row when every key:value pair
# in the rule equals the row), then append includes.
result=$(jq '
  . as $cfg
  | ($cfg.axes     // {})   as $axes
  | ($cfg.exclude  // [])   as $excludes
  | ($cfg.include  // [])   as $includes
  | ($cfg["max-parallel"] // null) as $mp
  | ($cfg["fail-fast"]    // true) as $ff
  | ($cfg["max-size"]     // null) as $ms

  | ( if ($axes | length) == 0 then []
      else
        $axes
        | to_entries
        | reduce .[] as $e
            ( [{}];
              [ .[] as $acc
                | $e.value[] as $v
                | $acc + {($e.key): $v} ] )
      end
    ) as $combos

  | ( $combos
      | map( . as $row
             | select(
                 ($excludes
                   | map( . as $ex
                          | ([ $ex | to_entries[]
                               | $row[.key] == .value ] | all) )
                   | any) | not ) )
    ) as $filtered

  | ($filtered + $includes) as $entries
  | ($entries | length) as $count
  | if $ms != null and $count > $ms then
      { error: "matrix size \($count) exceeds max-size \($ms)",
        count: $count, "max-size": $ms }
    else
      { matrix: {include: $entries},
        count: $count,
        "max-parallel": $mp,
        "fail-fast": $ff }
    end
' <<<"$input")

echo "$result"

if jq -e '.error' >/dev/null 2>&1 <<<"$result"; then
    echo "Error: $(jq -r '.error' <<<"$result")" >&2
    exit 3
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# generate-matrix.sh — read a matrix config JSON and emit an expanded
# GitHub Actions build matrix as JSON.
#
# Config schema:
#   {
#     "axes":         { "<key>": ["v1","v2",...], ... },
#     "include":      [ {<key>: <val>, ...}, ... ],
#     "exclude":      [ {<key>: <val>, ...}, ... ],
#     "max-parallel": <int|null>,
#     "fail-fast":    <bool>,
#     "max-size":     <int|null>
#   }
#
# Output:
#   { "matrix": {"include": [...]}, "count": N,
#     "max-parallel": ..., "fail-fast": ... }
# Exit codes: 0 ok, 2 bad input, 3 matrix exceeds max-size.
set -euo pipefail

usage() { echo "usage: $0 <config.json>   (or pipe JSON to stdin with -)" >&2; }

src="${1:-}"
if [[ -z "$src" ]]; then
    usage; exit 2
fi
if [[ "$src" == "-" ]]; then
    input="$(cat)"
elif [[ -f "$src" ]]; then
    input="$(cat "$src")"
else
    echo "Error: config file not found: $src" >&2
    exit 2
fi

if ! jq -e . >/dev/null 2>&1 <<<"$input"; then
    echo "Error: invalid JSON in config" >&2
    exit 2
fi
if ! jq -e '(.axes // null) | (type == "object") or (. == null)' >/dev/null <<<"$input"; then
    echo "Error: 'axes' must be an object" >&2
    exit 2
fi

# The jq program below does the real work: cartesian product of axes,
# apply excludes (an exclude rule matches a row when every key:value pair
# in the rule equals the row), then append includes.
result=$(jq '
  . as $cfg
  | ($cfg.axes     // {})   as $axes
  | ($cfg.exclude  // [])   as $excludes
  | ($cfg.include  // [])   as $includes
  | ($cfg["max-parallel"] // null) as $mp
  | ($cfg["fail-fast"]    // true) as $ff
  | ($cfg["max-size"]     // null) as $ms

  | ( if ($axes | length) == 0 then []
      else
        $axes
        | to_entries
        | reduce .[] as $e
            ( [{}];
              [ .[] as $acc
                | $e.value[] as $v
                | $acc + {($e.key): $v} ] )
      end
    ) as $combos

  | ( $combos
      | map( . as $row
             | select(
                 ($excludes
                   | map( . as $ex
                          | ([ $ex | to_entries[]
                               | $row[.key] == .value ] | all) )
                   | any) | not ) )
    ) as $filtered

  | ($filtered + $includes) as $entries
  | ($entries | length) as $count
  | if $ms != null and $count > $ms then
      { error: "matrix size \($count) exceeds max-size \($ms)",
        count: $count, "max-size": $ms }
    else
      { matrix: {include: $entries},
        count: $count,
        "max-parallel": $mp,
        "fail-fast": $ff }
    end
' <<<"$input")

echo "$result"

if jq -e '.error' >/dev/null 2>&1 <<<"$result"; then
    echo "Error: $(jq -r '.error' <<<"$result")" >&2
    exit 3
fi

content.text: Good, jq is available. Now writing the workflow.

input.content:
name: environment-matrix-generator
on:
  push:
  pull_request:
  workflow_dispatch:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 6 * * 1'

permissions:
  contents: read

env:
  CONFIG_FILE: matrix-config.json

jobs:
  generate-matrix:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Ensure jq is available
        shell: bash
        run: |
          if ! command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            apt-get update && apt-get install -y jq
          fi
          jq --version

      - name: Run matrix generator over all cases
        shell: bash
        env:
          CONFIG_FILE: ${{ env.CONFIG_FILE }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          chmod +x ./generate-matrix.sh

          if [[ ! -f "$CONFIG_FILE" ]]; then
            echo "No $CONFIG_FILE found — skipping" >&2
            exit 0
          fi

          cases_len=$(jq '.cases | length' "$CONFIG_FILE")
          echo "Running $cases_len matrix case(s)"

          pass=0
          for i in $(seq 0 $((cases_len - 1))); do
            name=$(jq -r ".cases[$i].name" "$CONFIG_FILE")
            jq ".cases[$i].config"  "$CONFIG_FILE" > case-config.json
            jq ".cases[$i].expect"  "$CONFIG_FILE" > case-expect.json

            echo "--- case: $name ---"
            set +e
            out=$(./generate-matrix.sh case-config.json 2>case-err.txt)
            rc=$?
            set -e
            echo "$out"
            echo "exit=$rc"

            want_error=$(jq -r '.error // empty' case-expect.json)
            if [[ -n "$want_error" ]]; then
              if [[ $rc -ne 3 ]]; then
                echo "FAIL: case '$name' expected error exit 3, got $rc"; exit 1
              fi
              echo "CASE-OK $name error"
              pass=$((pass+1)); continue
            fi

            if [[ $rc -ne 0 ]]; then
              echo "FAIL: case '$name' exited $rc"; cat case-err.txt; exit 1
            fi

            want_count=$(jq -r '.count'         case-expect.json)
            got_count=$(jq  -r '.count'         <<<"$out")
            if [[ "$want_count" != "null" && "$got_count" != "$want_count" ]]; then
              echo "FAIL: case '$name' count: want=$want_count got=$got_count"; exit 1
            fi

            line="CASE-OK $name count=$got_count"

            want_extra=$(jq -r '."has-extra" // false' case-expect.json)
            if [[ "$want_extra" == "true" ]]; then
              got_extra=$(jq '[.matrix.include[] | select(.extra=="x")] | length' <<<"$out")
              if [[ "$got_extra" -lt 1 ]]; then
                echo "FAIL: case '$name' expected include with extra=x"; exit 1
              fi
              line="$line extra=yes"
            fi

            want_mp=$(jq -r '."max-parallel" // empty' case-expect.json)
            if [[ -n "$want_mp" ]]; then
              got_mp=$(jq -r '."max-parallel"' <<<"$out")
              if [[ "$got_mp" != "$want_mp" ]]; then
                echo "FAIL: case '$name' max-parallel: want=$want_mp got=$got_mp"; exit 1
              fi
              line="$line max-parallel=$got_mp"
            fi

            want_ff=$(jq -r '."fail-fast" // empty' case-expect.json)
            if [[ -n "$want_ff" ]]; then
              got_ff=$(jq -r '."fail-fast"' <<<"$out")
              if [[ "$got_ff" != "$want_ff" ]]; then
                echo "FAIL: case '$name' fail-fast: want=$want_ff got=$got_ff"; exit 1
              fi
              line="$line fail-fast=$got_ff"
            fi

            echo "$line"
            pass=$((pass+1))
          done

          echo "Passed: $pass / $cases_len"
          if [[ "$pass" -eq "$cases_len" ]]; then
            echo "ALL-CASES-PASSED"
          else
            echo "SOME-CASES-FAILED"; exit 1
          fi

All 6 bats tests pass — one `act push` run executed all 6 matrix cases end-to-end. Deliverables:

- `generate-matrix.sh` — Bash+jq generator: cartesian expansion, include/exclude, max-parallel, fail-fast, max-size validation (exits 3 on overflow)
- `tests/matrix.bats` — TDD tests: actionlint, workflow structure, shellcheck, `bash -n`, and full act run with exact-value assertions (counts, extras, max-parallel, fail-fast, error case)
- `.github/workflows/environment-matrix-generator.yml` — push/PR/schedule/workflow_dispatch, iterates cases, emits `CASE-OK ...` and `ALL-CASES-PASSED`
- `act-result.txt` — captured output containing all `CASE-OK` lines, `ALL-CASES-PASSED`, and `Job succeeded`
